items.title: The AI Memory Supercycle Could Last Longer Than Investors Expect. That's Great News for Micron and Sandisk.
items.summary: Micron's Q3 FY2026 earnings significantly exceeded expectations with $41.5B in revenue versus $33.5B guidance, suggesting the AI-driven memory chip supercycle is still in early stages and likely to extend longer than anticipated. The company's record-level investments and multiyear customer agreements indicate sustained demand, while Sandisk is expected to report similarly strong results. These developments suggest the memory supercycle will maintain elevated prices and high margins for extended periods.

items.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Stock Is Plunging. Should You Buy the Dip?
items.summary: SpaceX shares have fallen nearly 20% from their June 16 peak after surging 67% at IPO. Despite the dip, the author recommends against buying, citing an extremely high price-to-sales ratio of 77 (compared to Nvidia's 20 and Micron's 24), lack of profitability, and concerns about planned debt and equity dilution. The stock is priced for perfection with no margin of safety.

items.title: Micron Reports Earnings June 24. Here's the One Number That May Make or Break the Stock.
items.summary: Micron Technology will report fiscal Q3 earnings on June 24, with gross margin being the critical metric to watch. The company guided for 81% gross margin, reflecting strong AI-driven pricing power in memory chips. However, with shares up 244% in 2026 and trading at mid-40s P/E ratios, the stock faces risks from potential margin compression if supply catches up to demand or if the pricing cycle peaks.
